reason: Applications can recreate cache content when needed.
impact: The source application may open more slowly while it recreates or downloads this cache.
evidence: Matched a top-level directory inside the user cache root.
whatItIs: Temporary data stored by an application to avoid repeating work.

reason: Old diagnostic logs are usually no longer needed for current troubleshooting.
impact: Historical diagnostic information will no longer be available; applications may create new logs.
evidence: Matched an item in the user log root and passed the configured age threshold.
whatItIs: Diagnostic output written by an application or developer tool.

reason: Logs can often be recreated but recent logs may be useful for troubleshooting.
impact: Recent diagnostic information will be removed and may not be reproducible.
evidence: Matched an item in the user log root that is newer than the safe-age threshold.
whatItIs: Recent diagnostic output from an application or tool.

reason: The item is already in Trash but permanent removal requires a separate decision.
impact: Normal cleanup does not act on this item; the Trash page can permanently remove it only after separate confirmation.
whatItIs: An item currently present in the current user’s Trash.

reason: Xcode can regenerate build intermediates during the next build.
impact: The next build of the affected project may take longer.
evidence: Matched the known Xcode DerivedData directory.
whatItIs: Indexes, build products, and intermediate files generated by Xcode.

reason: Old archives may be unnecessary, but can contain irreplaceable signed builds and debug symbols.
impact: The archived build and its symbols will no longer be available in Xcode Organizer unless restored.
evidence: Matched the known Xcode Archives directory.
whatItIs: Packaged application builds and debug symbols retained by Xcode Organizer.

reason: Xcode can reacquire device support files, but developers may still need specific OS versions.
impact: Connecting or debugging a device on that OS version may require Xcode to prepare support files again.
evidence: Matched the known Xcode iOS DeviceSupport directory.
whatItIs: Symbols and support files used to debug physical Apple devices.

reason: Simulator caches are regeneratable, but may be in use by active simulator services.
impact: This release reports simulator cache usage but does not clean it, because active-use detection is not sufficiently reliable.
evidence: Matched the CoreSimulator cache root, not a simulator device data directory.
whatItIs: Regeneratable metadata and caches used by CoreSimulator.

reason: Homebrew can download cached packages again when required.
impact: Future Homebrew operations may need to download packages again.
evidence: Matched the known Homebrew download-cache root.
whatItIs: Downloaded bottles, source archives, and API cache data retained by Homebrew.

reason: npm can download package artifacts again.
impact: A later npm install may need to download packages again.
whatItIs: Downloaded package artifacts retained by npm; installed dependencies and project files are excluded.

reason: Yarn can download cached package artifacts again.
impact: A later Yarn install may need to download packages again.
evidence: Matched Yarn’s known user cache root.
whatItIs: Downloaded dependency artifacts retained by Yarn; project dependencies and lockfiles are excluded.

reason: The pnpm store can be repopulated, but active projects may link package content from it.
impact: Projects may need packages downloaded again; review before removing the shared store.
evidence: Matched pnpm’s known shared store root.
whatItIs: A shared content-addressable package store used by pnpm.

reason: pip can download and rebuild cached package artifacts again.
impact: A later pip install may need to download or rebuild packages.
whatItIs: Downloaded Python packages and locally built wheel artifacts retained by pip.

reason: Conda package caches may be redownloaded, but environments can share or link package data.
impact: Existing environments may rely on linked data and future operations may redownload packages; use Conda’s own cleanup command when possible.
evidence: Matched the user-level Conda package-cache root.
whatItIs: Downloaded and extracted packages managed by Conda, excluding environment directories.

reason: A disk image or installer package may no longer be needed after installation.
impact: You will need to download the installer again if it is needed later.
evidence: Matched a recognized .dmg or .pkg installer extension in an authorized Downloads root.
whatItIs: A downloaded macOS disk image or installer package.

reason: The file exceeds the configured size threshold; size alone does not make it unnecessary.
impact: The selected file will leave its current location. Confirm its contents and backup status first.
evidence: Found by metadata analysis in an explicitly approved location.
whatItIs: A user file above the configured analysis threshold.

reason: Old local device backups can be removed after confirming another usable backup exists.
impact: This removes the complete local backup for that device and date; its internals are never modified individually.
evidence: Matched a complete backup directory in Apple’s MobileSync backup root.
whatItIs: A local backup of an iPhone or iPad.
